login.hero_alt: Scene from the movie "A Beautiful Mind"

hero.title: Use cinema to teach
hero.description: Telapsi is an academic platform designed to support teachers in creating lessons with the help of movies.

about.title: About Telapsi
about.description: Free academic project to support teachers in teaching psychiatry using movies pedagogically.
about.ethical_description: Telapsi values the use of non-stigmatizing language, clinical context, and references to guidelines.

planning.title: Lesson Plans
planning.no_plans: You don't have any planned lessons yet.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Due to copyright issues, Telapsi is unable to store or play excerpts or full movies.
faq.a2: Telapsi is an academic project and, therefore, non-profit. Access and use of the platform are completely free.
faq.q3: Can I export PDF or PPTX?
faq.a3: It is possible to export a teaching guide or a movie file in PDF, but not in PPTX.
faq.q4: How is my data being used?
faq.a4: Telapsi stores only your e-mail address for authentication purposes. No other data is collected.

footer.disclaimer: LEGAL NOTICE – COPYRIGHT: The TELAPSI platform does not host, reproduce, transmit, make available for download or share clips of audiovisual works protected by copyright, including movies, series, documentaries or any cinematographic content. All audiovisual content used in pedagogical activities must be accessed by the user through legally licensed services, such as streaming platforms, physical media purchased, digital rental shops or other means permitted by Brazilian legislation. The analyses, descriptions, teaching guides, lesson plans, pedagogical comments, temporal references (timecodes) and other materials made available on TELAPSI constitute independent intellectual work, protected by Law No. 9,610/1998, and are intended exclusively for academic and educational purposes. Any showing of scenes or movies in teaching environments must respect current legal provisions, including the use of legally acquired copies and the observance of the limitations provided for in copyright legislation. The responsibility for access to the original audiovisual content is exclusively the user's.
